title: Tangy Flaky French Cheese Omelette
description: A quick, paleofriendly French omelette with tangy lemoninfused eggs, generous cheese slices, and a delicate flaky texture achieved by gentle steam folding.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs with salt, pepper, lemon juice, and water until the mixture is smooth and slightly frothy.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑low heat and add the tablespoon of olive oil, swirling to coat.
Pour the whisked eggs into the skillet, tilting the pan to spread them evenly. Let the eggs sit undisturbed for 30–45 seconds until the edges begin to set.
Place the cheddar cheese slices on the surface of the still‑liquid eggs, allowing the cheese to melt slightly.
Gently slide a spatula around the edges to lift the cooked portions and let raw egg flow underneath.
Cover the skillet with a lid and let the omelette steam for 2–3 minutes, which creates a flaky, airy interior.
Once the top is set but still slightly translucent, fold the omelette in half or roll it, then slide it onto a plate.
Sprinkle chopped parsley over the top for a fresh finish and serve immediately.
